words.definition: A story told through moving pictures, usually shown in a cinema or on a screen
words.example: Let's go see a movie tonight — there's a new comedy showing.

words.definition: A person who performs a role in a film or TV show
words.example: My favorite actor won an award for his role in that drama.

words.definition: A building where films are shown to a paying audience on a large screen
words.example: We bought tickets online and went to the cinema on Saturday night.

words.definition: A set of TV episodes that follow the same story or characters across multiple seasons
words.example: I started a new crime series last night and I'm already hooked.

words.definition: A single part of a film or show that takes place in one location at one time
words.example: The opening scene of the film is absolutely breathtaking.

words.definition: Watching or listening to digital media online without downloading it
words.example: I watched the whole documentary series streaming on my phone.

words.definition: A short promotional preview of a film or television series
words.example: Have you seen the trailer for the new superhero film yet?

words.definition: The sequence of events that make up the main story of a film or series
words.example: The plot of the novel was full of surprising twists.

words.definition: A film or TV programme that presents facts about real events or people
words.example: The documentary explores the long-term impact of climate change.

words.definition: An arrangement to pay regularly in exchange for access to a service
words.example: I have a monthly subscription to a popular streaming platform.

words.definition: To watch many episodes of a series one after another in a short time
words.example: I binge-watched the entire first season over the weekend.

words.definition: A category of films or series defined by a shared style or subject matter
words.example: Mystery is her favourite film genre.

words.definition: A film or series that continues the story of an earlier one
words.example: The sequel was even more exciting than the original film.

words.definition: The recorded music that plays during a film or television show
words.example: The movie's powerful soundtrack won three international awards.

words.definition: The first public showing of a new film, usually attended by the cast and crew
words.example: The film's glamorous premiere was held in Los Angeles.

words.definition: The complete group of actors appearing in a film or series
words.example: The film has an incredible cast of internationally famous actors.

words.definition: The main character of a story, film, or series
words.example: The protagonist faces many moral dilemmas throughout the story.

words.definition: Praised enthusiastically and widely by critics and the public
words.example: The director's latest film has been widely acclaimed by critics.

words.definition: The spoken words exchanged between characters in a film or script
words.example: The sharp dialogue between the two leads made every scene crackle with tension.

words.definition: A film that is very commercially successful and attracts huge audiences
words.example: The studio invested heavily in the blockbuster, hoping it would dominate the summer box office.

words.definition: A secondary storyline that runs alongside and supports the main plot of a film
words.example: The romantic subplot felt underdeveloped and distracted from the main story.

words.definition: The art and technique of filming a movie, including camera angles, lighting, and composition
words.example: The film won an Oscar for Best Cinematography thanks to its stunning visual style.

words.definition: The way a story is structured and told, including the order of events and the perspective chosen
words.example: The film's non-linear narrative keeps the audience guessing until the final scene.

words.definition: The speed at which a film's story develops, affecting how tense or slow it feels
words.example: Critics praised the slow pacing of the first act, which built tension effectively.

words.definition: The written script of a film, including dialogue, scene descriptions, and action
words.example: She won the award for Best Original Screenplay at the film festival.

words.definition: A director whose personal artistic vision is strongly reflected throughout their films
words.example: She is considered an auteur because every film she makes reflects her distinct style.

words.definition: A story in which characters and events represent deeper moral or political meanings
words.example: The film is widely read as an allegory for racial inequality in America.

words.definition: Hidden meanings or themes in a film that are implied but not stated directly
words.example: The subtext of the film is a critique of capitalism disguised as a romantic comedy.

words.definition: All the visual elements arranged within a film frame, including sets, lighting, costumes, and actor placement
words.example: The director's meticulous mise-en-scène makes every frame feel deliberately composed.

words.definition: The final resolution of a story, where loose ends are tied up after the climax
words.example: The film's dénouement was ambiguous, leaving audiences to interpret the ending themselves.

words.definition: A work that strongly argues against something, often a social or political system
words.example: The documentary was seen as a polemic against the pharmaceutical industry.
